_source: Synthetic representative profile for Rossignol 2012 Med Gas Res REVIEW (PMID 22703610). Per cohort.yaml notes, this entry is a REVIEW of HBOT-in-ASD evidence and is EXCLUDED from cohort responder-rate MAE because no dichotomized stratum responder rate is published at the review level. The representative profile is authored for engine STRUCTURAL-TEST purposes only — encoding the mito-vulnerable + neuroinflammatory subset that Rossignol 2012 explicitly hypothesizes (page 9) as the responder phenotype: 'children with abnormal cytokines, higher inflammatory markers, cerebral hypoperfusion, or mitochondrial dysfunction may be more likely to demonstrate improvements.' Engine should load PHE-0002 + PHE-0003 (mito + immune-inflammatory) on this profile.

organic_acids_urine._mito_dysfunction_marker_per_rossignol_s: Rossignol 2012 cites mito dysfunction in 145 ASD publications and notes HBOT may improve mito function specifically in this subset

_engine_expectation.rationale: Rossignol 2012 review explicitly hypothesizes a mito-vulnerable + neuroinflammatory subset as the HBOT responder phenotype. This representative profile encodes elevated lactate (mito proxy) + elevated IL-6 + TNF (neuroinflammation proxy). Engine should load PHE-0002 (mito) primary + PHE-0003 (immune-inflammatory) secondary. EXCLUDED from cohort MAE because the source is a review and does not publish a dichotomized stratum responder rate.
